english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Galatians

verses.latin_vulgate: Fratres, etsi præoccupatus fuerit homo in aliquo delicto, vos, qui spirituales estis, hujusmodi instruite in spiritu lenitatis, considerans teipsum, ne et tu tenteris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀδελφοί ἐὰν καὶ προληφθῇ ἄνθρωπος ἔν τινι παραπτώματι ὑμεῖς οἱ πνευματικοὶ καταρτίζετε τὸν τοιοῦτον ἐν πνεύματι πρᾳότητος, σκοπῶν σεαυτόν μὴ καὶ σὺ πειρασθῇς
verses.king_james: Brethren, if a man be overtaken in a fault, ye which are spiritual, restore such an one in the spirit of meekness; considering thyself, lest thou also be tempted.

verses.latin_vulgate: Alter alterius onera portate, et sic adimplebitis legem Christi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλήλων τὰ βάρη βαστάζετε καὶ οὕτως ἀναπληρώσατε τὸν νόμον τοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: Bear ye one another's burdens, and so fulfil the law of Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam si quis existimat se aliquid esse, cum nihil sit, ipse se seducit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ γὰρ δοκεῖ τις εἶναί τι μηδὲν ὤν ἑαυτόν φρεναπατᾷ
verses.king_james: For if a man think himself to be something, when he is nothing, he deceiveth himself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Opus autem suum probet unusquisque, et sic in semetipso tantum gloriam habebit, et non in altero.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ δὲ ἔργον ἑαυτοῦ δοκιμαζέτω ἕκαστος καὶ τότε εἰς ἑαυτὸν μόνον τὸ καύχημα ἕξει καὶ οὐκ εἰς τὸν ἕτερον
verses.king_james: But let every man prove his own work, and then shall he have rejoicing in himself alone, and not in another.

verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕκαστος γὰρ τὸ ἴδιον φορτίον βαστάσει
verses.king_james: For every man shall bear his own burden.

verses.latin_vulgate: Communicet autem is qui catechizatur verbo, ei qui se catechizat, in omnibus bonis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κοινωνείτω δὲ ὁ κατηχούμενος τὸν λόγον τῷ κατηχοῦντι ἐν πᾶσιν ἀγαθοῖς
verses.king_james: Let him that is taught in the word communicate unto him that teacheth in all good things.

verses.greek_textus_receptus: Μὴ πλανᾶσθε θεὸς οὐ μυκτηρίζεται ὃ γὰρ ἐὰν σπείρῃ ἄνθρωπος τοῦτο καὶ θερίσει
verses.king_james: Be not deceived; God is not mocked: for whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ enim seminaverit homo, hæc et metet. Quoniam qui seminat in carne sua, de carne et metet corruptionem: qui autem seminat in spiritu, de spiritu metet vitam æternam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι ὁ σπείρων εἰς τὴν σάρκα ἑαυτοῦ ἐκ τῆς σαρκὸς θερίσει φθοράν ὁ δὲ σπείρων εἰς τὸ πνεῦμα ἐκ τοῦ πνεύματος θερίσει ζωὴν αἰώνιον
verses.king_james: For he that soweth to his flesh shall of the flesh reap corruption; but he that soweth to the Spirit shall of the Spirit reap life everlasting.

verses.latin_vulgate: Bonum autem facientes, non deficiamus: tempore enim suo metemus non deficientes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ δὲ καλὸν ποιοῦντες μὴ ἐκκακῶμεν καιρῷ γὰρ ἰδίῳ θερίσομεν μὴ ἐκλυόμενοι
verses.king_james: And let us not be weary in well doing: for in due season we shall reap, if we faint not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ergo dum tempus habemus, operemur bonum ad omnes, maxime autem ad domesticos fidei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄρα οὖν ὡς καιρὸν ἔχομεν ἐργαζώμεθα τὸ ἀγαθὸν πρὸς πάντας μάλιστα δὲ πρὸς τοὺς οἰκείους τῆς πίστεως
verses.king_james: As we have therefore opportunity, let us do good unto all men, especially unto them who are of the household of faith.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videte qualibus litteris scripsi vobis mea manu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἴδετε πηλίκοις ὑμῖν γράμμασιν ἔγραψα τῇ ἐμῇ χειρί
verses.king_james: Ye see how large a letter I have written unto you with mine own hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quicumque enim volunt placere in carne, hi cogunt vos circumcidi, tantum ut crucis Christi persecutionem non patiantur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅσοι θέλουσιν εὐπροσωπῆσαι ἐν σαρκί οὗτοι ἀναγκάζουσιν ὑμᾶς περιτέμνεσθαι μόνον ἵνα μὴ τῷ σταυρῷ τοῦ χριστοῦ διώκωνται
verses.king_james: As many as desire to make a fair shew in the flesh, they constrain you to be circumcised; only lest they should suffer persecution for the cross of Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Neque enim qui circumciduntur, legem custodiunt: sed volunt vos circumcidi, ut in carne vestra glorientur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδὲ γὰρ οἱ περιτεμνόμενοι αὐτοὶ νόμον φυλάσσουσιν ἀλλὰ θέλουσιν ὑμᾶς περιτέμνεσθαι ἵνα ἐν τῇ ὑμετέρᾳ σαρκὶ καυχήσωνται
verses.king_james: For neither they themselves who are circumcised keep the law; but desire to have you circumcised, that they may glory in your flesh.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mihi autem absit gloriari, nisi in cruce Domini nostri Jesu Christi: per quem mihi mundus crucifixus est, et ego mundo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐμοὶ δὲ μὴ γένοιτο καυχᾶσθαι εἰ μὴ ἐν τῷ σταυρῷ τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ δι᾽ οὗ ἐμοὶ κόσμος ἐσταύρωται κἀγὼ τῷ κόσμῳ
verses.king_james: But God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom the world is crucified unto me, and I unto the world.

verses.latin_vulgate: In Christo enim Jesu neque circumcisio aliquid valet, neque præputium, sed nova creatura.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν γὰρ χριστῷ ἰησοῦ οὔτε περιτομή τί ἰσχύει, οὔτε ἀκροβυστία ἀλλὰ καινὴ κτίσις
verses.king_james: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision availeth any thing, nor uncircumcision, but a new creature.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quicumque hanc regulam secuti fuerint, pax super illos, et misericordia, et super Israël Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὅσοι τῷ κανόνι τούτῳ στοιχήσουσιν εἰρήνη ἐπ᾽ αὐτοὺς καὶ ἔλεος καὶ ἐπὶ τὸν ἰσραὴλ τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And as many as walk according to this rule, peace be on them, and mercy, and upon the Israel of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: De cetero, nemo mihi molestus sit: ego enim stigmata Domini Jesu in corpore meo porto.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ λοιποῦ κόπους μοι μηδεὶς παρεχέτω ἐγὼ γὰρ τὰ στίγματα τοῦ κυριοῦ ἰησοῦ ἐν τῷ σώματί μου βαστάζω
verses.king_james: From henceforth let no man trouble me: for I bear in my body the marks of the Lord Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gratia Domini nostri Jesu Christi cum spiritu vestro, fratres. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ χάρις τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ μετὰ τοῦ πνεύματος ὑμῶν ἀδελφοί ἀμήν πρός γαλάτας ἐγράφη ἀπό ῥώμης
verses.king_james: Brethren, the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
